content.full_text:
Independent Expenditures Are Not Late Contributions

An independent expenditure which occurs after the closing date for the second pre-election statement is not a late contribution. This is because late contributions apply only to cash or in-kind contributions and not independent expenditures.

Opinion Request Meeting, May 30, 1978.

sections.question_synthetic: Whether independent expenditures made after the closing date for the second pre-election statement constitute late contributions under campaign finance law.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: Independent expenditures are not subject to late contribution restrictions because late contribution rules apply only to cash or in-kind contributions, not independent expenditures.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Whether independent expenditures made after the closing date for the second pre-election statement constitute late contributions under campaign finance law.

CONCLUSION: An independent expenditure which occurs after the closing date for the second pre-election statement is not a late contribution. This is because late contributions apply only to cash or in-kind contributions and not independent expenditures.

embedding.summary: FPPC advice letter clarifying that independent expenditures made after the second pre-election statement closing date do not constitute late contributions, as late contribution rules apply only to cash or in-kind contributions.
